learningAndDecisions.title: Exit Annuity Strategy — Removed from 2025–2030 Plan
learningAndDecisions.description:
After a thorough review in Q1 2026, leadership made the decision to remove the annuity product line from Wellabe's 2025–2030 strategic plan. The decision was not reactive — it reflected a deliberate reassessment of the risk-adjusted return profile of annuities relative to our core products in the current and projected interest rate environment, and a clear-eyed view of where Wellabe has durable competitive advantages.

The interest rate context matters. Annuity products — particularly MYGAs and FIAs — derive their attractiveness to consumers from the spread between crediting rates and Wellabe's investment yields. As interest rates have shifted from the environment modeled in the original plan, the economics of the MYGA product (which were already highly sensitive to ceding commission and scale) became significantly less favorable. A 5bps change in ceding commission flips MYGA IRR from marginally positive to negative. The margin of safety was too thin for a product line in which Wellabe was still building internal expertise.

Equally important was a reaffirmation of where Wellabe's competitive advantages actually lie. Our core products — Medicare Supplement, ancillary health, and Preneed — benefit from decades of underwriting knowledge, established distribution relationships, brand trust, and actuarial depth. These are defensible positions. Annuities required building new capabilities in ALM, hedging, FIA pricing, and third-party administrator management essentially from scratch, while simultaneously depending on reinsurance partners for capital relief and distribution partners for sales volume. That level of execution dependency on external parties, in a product category where Wellabe had limited institutional knowledge, represented a concentration of strategic risk that was not commensurate with the expected return.

The capital consideration was decisive. Medicare Supplement growth alone is projected to consume over $150M in capital support between 2025 and 2030. Adding annuity capital requirements — even in a capital-light reinsurance structure — would have compounded RBC pressure at precisely the moment when capital flexibility is most critical. Removing annuities allows the organization to direct its financial and operational resources toward the businesses where it has the highest conviction.

The growth strategy has been updated accordingly. All references to MYGA, FIA, and annuity partnership structures have been removed from planning documents and KPI frameworks.
learningAndDecisions.impact: Growth strategy reoriented around Medicare Supplement, ancillary health, and Preneed. Capital plan simplified — annuity-related RBC strain removed from projections. Distribution and technology investments refocused on core product digital experiences. ALM and annuity-specific actuarial build-out deferred indefinitely.

learningAndDecisions.title: Surplus Note Designated as Primary Capital Solution
learningAndDecisions.owner: Sara Lehan, Jeff Harms, Scott Haugh
learningAndDecisions.description:
Following a structured review of available capital management levers, Wellabe's leadership designated the issuance of a surplus note — targeting approximately $100M — as the organization's primary capital solution for resolving the projected RBC shortfall.

The decision was made through the capital framework developed in 2025 and used for the 26R2 plan, which evaluates capital instruments across six dimensions: expected impact on RBC, cost of capital, effect on product returns, governance and regulatory complexity, speed to execution, and strategic control implications. The surplus note performed most favorably across this framework.

Why a surplus note and not an alternative? Surplus notes are subordinated debt instruments that qualify as regulatory capital under insurance accounting rules — a distinction that makes them uniquely valuable in the insurance context. Unlike traditional debt, a surplus note improves RBC directly. Unlike quota share reinsurance, it does not cede any portion of future product returns or require Wellabe to share underwriting risk management authority with a counterparty. Unlike an equity transaction or agency sale, it does not involve permanent ownership dilution or the monetization of a long-term asset at what may be a suboptimal moment.

The cost of capital is also relatively attractive. At the interest rates currently available to Wellabe — given its AM Best A (Excellent) rating — a surplus note represents one of the cheapest forms of external capital accessible to a mutual insurer. The annual interest expense, while real, is modest relative to the embedded value that Medicare Supplement growth is expected to generate over the 2025–2030 period. In other words, the business case is clear: the cost of the note is far lower than the cost of slowing MS sales to protect RBC organically.

RBC Capital Markets has been engaged to advise on the issuance. Key execution steps are: completing the preliminary sizing analysis, identifying likely note purchasers among institutional fixed-income investors, obtaining Iowa Insurance Division regulatory approval, and executing the transaction. Estimated timeline to execution: 9–15 months from engagement. The primary risk to the surplus note track is regulatory approval timing — surplus note issuance requires explicit prior approval from the domiciliary regulator, and that process is not fully predictable.

The FE block sale and MS quota share reinsurance remain active secondary tracks and will be advanced in parallel. If the surplus note faces regulatory delay, these tracks provide execution optionality. However, leadership's preference — and the track being given primary resource attention — is the surplus note.
learningAndDecisions.impact: RBC Capital Markets formally engaged for ~$100M issuance. FE block sale and quota share reinsurance maintained as parallel secondary tracks. Capital Opportunities Playbook to be updated to reflect this prioritization and the decision rationale for future reference. CapCo to receive quarterly updates on execution progress. The RBC KPI remains red until a capital solution reaches sufficient certainty of execution.
